preamble: The shift from the rhotic consonant [r] to the mid-front vowel [e] is a specific regional variant of r-vocalization. While vocalization typically results in central or back vowels, in certain Central Germanic dialects, the rhotic quality is replaced by a fronted vocalic off-glide, particularly after other front vowels.

examples.note: Final r vocalizes into a fronted vowel quality.

sources: Waterman: A History of the German Language
